projects.name: TEMPLATE — copy this for a new bid

tasks.activity: Kick off meeting — confirm scope, reverse brief, budget shape, task list
Hours/cost table, insurance, ABN, rate card confirmed
Value add, CVs, subcontractors, referees finalised
Final export, compliance check, upload, receipt saved

projects.name: Embedding Climate Risk into Governance & Financial Decision-Making
projects.client: LGA South Australia (co-funded: LGA Mutual, Asset Mutual Fund, Local Government Risk Services, LGFA, SA Local Govt Financial Mgmt Group, SA Local Govt Auditors Group)
projects.deliveryWindow: July – November 2026 (5 month delivery window if awarded)

reverseBrief.situation: LGA South Australia has issued RFQ ECM 866093 (Tenders SA code LGA058959), seeking a consultant to design a practical, scalable approach that embeds climate-related risk into governance, planning, asset management and financial decision-making across its 68 member councils. Haskoning has decided to bid.
reverseBrief.complication: Nine working days from kick-off (Mon 6 Jul) to lodgement (2pm Thu 16 Jul 2026). Proposal development budget capped at $3,500. Stella must define methodology before the narrative can be written. Tenders SA categorises the tender 75% across corporate finance / risk management / financial asset management vs 25% environmental advisory — the proposal must read as financial governance work, not a climate report.
reverseBrief.question: How does Haskoning sequence a compliant, competitive, full proposal within nine working days and a $3,500 development budget, with clearly owned tasks and hours for each team member?
reverseBrief.answer: Run a five-phase, gated sequence anchored on assets Haskoning already holds: Stella's draft nine-stage method (DEFINE, DESIGN, BUILD, VALIDATE, INTEGRATE, DELIVER & ENABLE) and the Commonwealth CROMP digital risk tool, adapted rather than built from scratch. Close the $20k gap between the draft method's stage budgets ($90k) and the RFQ's $70k cap first, then draft (Katrina/Lien), commercial input in parallel (Zoe/Alan), one QA pass (Wed 15 Jul), lodge by 12 noon Thu 16 Jul.
keyMessages: The methodology largely exists — Stella's draft nine-stage method maps cleanly to the RFQ's required deliverables, and the CROMP tool provides a Commonwealth-endorsed toolkit backbone, strengthening the 40% methodology score and de-risking the timeline.

The draft method is at least $20k over budget — stage budgets total $90,000 against the $70,000 ex GST cap with no profit or contingency. If Haskoning's standard on-cost stack (PA4540: 5% contingency, 3% profit, 10% PM) applies, the effective gap is $31k. Pricing basis, then rescope, is the decision sequence for kick-off.

Position the bid as financial governance work — the 75% corporate finance/risk/asset weighting on Tenders SA plays to the gap CROMP leaves open (financial consequence translation), which is exactly where Haskoning differentiates.
scopeIn: Practical tools embedding climate risk into risk registers, asset management plans, long-term financial plans, strategic planning and annual reporting
A tiered pathway — accessible baseline for resource-constrained councils, advanced pathway toward AASB S2 / IPSASB SRS1 alignment
Structured engagement: Steering Group (monthly), CEO Advisory Group session, one cross-functional workshop, testing with regional/lower-capacity councils
Leadership and governance material — concise briefing content for executives and elected members
scopeOut: Detailed climate modelling or new climate projections
Development of a full climate disclosure compliance framework

constraints.detail: $70,000 excluding GST for the delivery contract (separate to the $3,500 proposal development budget)
Minimum one session — LGA notes Thursday 6 August as a possible slot
One cross-functional workshop covering risk, finance, asset management, sustainability and governance functions
Draft outputs tested with regional or lower-capacity councils before finalisation
Outputs owned by LGA South Australia on completion; Haskoning may reference in portfolio but cannot commercialise without prior approval

openQuestions.text: Budget gap: draft method totals $90k vs $70k ex GST cap. Which stages absorb the $20k reduction? (illustrative rescope: baseline assessment and capability building take the largest cuts)

CROMP positioning: name CROMP explicitly as the adapted foundation? Adaptation story (SA localisation, financial consequence depth, tiering for low-capacity councils) must be explicit so the bid doesn't read as reselling a free government tool.
On-cost treatment: standard PA4540 stack (contingency 5% + profit 3% + PM 10% = 18%) vs at-cost. Determines whether labour envelope is ~$59k or $70k — Alan to confirm.
Nominated personnel: confirm lead consultant and project team beyond Stella/Katrina; is Lien nominated personnel or internal support only? Public-facing hourly rates need Alan's sign-off.
Referees: up to three, ideally recent local government climate risk or financial sustainability work — confirm who Katrina should approach.

lodgement.tenderCode: LGA058959 on Tenders SA (internal ref ECM 866093) — verify both codes before uploading
lodgement.method: Tenders SA portal only, via "Click here to submit an electronic response". No direct email submissions. Standard formats only (Word/Excel/PDF); each upload ≤10MB — target a single PDF under 10MB, compress images/org charts.
lodgement.closingTime: 2:00 PM, Thursday 16 July 2026 (late quotations considered only at LGA's discretion). Internal target: upload complete by 12:00 noon for a 2-hour buffer.
lodgement.contact: Anna Rogers, Climate Change Support Advisor, LGA South Australia — all queries and communication must go through her in writing. Approaching other LGA staff risks disqualification.
lodgement.lastQueryDate: Thursday 16 July 2026 (same day as closing) — seek clarifications early next week, not in the final days.
lodgement.notes: Escalation if portal misbehaves: SA Tenders and Contracts Helpdesk, (08) 8462 1401. A successful upload produces an on-screen receipt and an emailed receipt number — save both immediately as proof of lodgement.

tasks.activity: Kick off meeting: pack reviewed, reverse brief confirmed, $20k budget gap decision made (rescope to $70k or $70k core + $20k Value Add), CROMP positioning agreed, task list locked
Refine and sign off the draft nine-stage method with agreed budget shape; confirm CROMP adaptation narrative and tiered pathway logic; hand to Katrina
Full proposal narrative: understanding of project, methodology write-up, integration into council systems, governance/leadership section
Research SA council examples, ESCOSA references; draft engagement section skeleton
Compile itemised hours/cost table for the RFQ budget schedule, support resourcing/personnel sections
Confirm insurance details, ABN, rate card and on-cost treatment for the $70k bid
Value add, project team CVs, subcontractors, referees finalised; full draft assembled
Review complete draft against evaluation criteria; incorporate final edits
tasks.comments: Single QA checkpoint — includes sub-10MB file size check

tasks.activity: Final PDF export (<10MB), compliance checklist re-run, upload against LGA058959 via Tenders SA, save receipt + receipt number email
tasks.comments: By 12 noon — 2 hr buffer before 2pm close

budget.resource: Stella Whittaker — refine/sign off methodology, budget decision, final QA
Katrina Rae — author full proposal, coordinate inputs, assemble & lodge
Lien — CROMP mapping, research, engagement draft, formatting

risks.risk: Draft method budget ($90k) exceeds the $70k ex GST cap — $20k gap at cost, up to $31k if standard on-cost stack applies
risks.mitigation: Decide at kick-off between Section 4.3 rescope and $70k core + $20k Value Add structure; Alan present or reachable for the commercial call

risks.risk: Bid reads as reselling a free Commonwealth tool (CROMP)
risks.mitigation: Lead methodology narrative with the adaptation/differentiation story: SA localisation, financial consequence depth, governance response for council instruments, tiering for low-capacity councils

risks.risk: Methodology sign-off slips past Tue 7 Jul
risks.mitigation: Protect as a critical path gate at kick-off; escalate immediately if at risk

risks.risk: On-cost basis unresolved — standard 18% stack would shrink labour envelope to ~$59k and deepen the rescope
risks.mitigation: Alan confirms on-cost treatment at or before kick-off, before stage rescoping is finalised
Stella to nominate up to three recent, relevant referees at kick-off so Lien can begin outreach early next week

risks.risk: Upload failure, oversize file or no receipt at lodgement
risks.mitigation: File size check in Wed QA (single PDF <10MB); upload by 12 noon Thu for a 2-hr buffer; retry on missing receipt; escalate to SA Tenders Helpdesk (08) 8462 1401; save on-screen receipt + email as proof

risks.risk: Proposal reads as an environmental report rather than financial governance work
risks.mitigation: Adopt 75% finance/risk/asset positioning at kick-off; Wed 15 Jul QA explicitly checks each section leads with financial sustainability language
